meta.description: Writes complete, publication-grade figure legends that can stand on their own. Use when writing or revising figure legends for any scientific figure.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated sample sizes, p-values, or statistical tests detected. Placeholder convention explicitly used for missing values.

practice_boundaries.detail: No clinical interpretations produced. Skill scope is figure legend writing only.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies. Statistical notation conventions correctly applied.

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Mode A text-output skill.

functional_suitability.note: Full marks. 11 figure types covered with type-specific additional elements; 8 template variants in legend_templates.md; journal-specific requirements for Nature, Cell, and Science journals included.

reliability.note: Placeholder convention prevents fabrication; Step 3 quality checklist with 7 verifiable criteria; no explicit handling for supplementary figure legends vs. main figure legends.

performance_context.note: Figure-type-specific elements table adds necessary but non-trivial token overhead; overall 3-step workflow is lean for the complexity covered.

agent_usability.note: Full marks. Type-specific elements table, standardized placeholder convention, journal-specific requirements, and per-panel multi-panel format all well-designed.

human_usability.note: Full marks. Description lists specific figure type names as triggers; immediately recognizable by any researcher needing to write a legend.

security.note: Full marks. Hard rules prohibit fabricated sample sizes, p-values, and statistical tests; placeholder convention enforces this at the output level.

maintainability.note: Both reference files (academic_style_guide.md, legend_templates.md) present and well-structured; journal-specific word limits and requirements will need periodic updates; no version tracking or 'last verified' date in reference files.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision via specific figure type names is excellent; placeholder composability enables downstream verification workflow; progressive disclosure could be more explicit for asking figure type before generating type-specific elements.

inputs.label: Bar chart: 3 groups (control, treatment A, treatment B), n=6/group, one-way ANOVA + Tukey, SEM error bars
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Complete legend using bar chart template; all required universal and type-specific elements present.

assertions.text: Legend includes figure number, brief title, and what each bar represents
assertions.note: Universal elements complete and correctly formatted.

assertions.text: Sample size (n=6 per group) is explicitly stated
assertions.note: N stated per group as required.

assertions.text: Statistical test (one-way ANOVA + Tukey) and significance thresholds are stated
assertions.note: Statistical notation matches user-provided test and standard threshold format.

assertions.text: Error bar type (SEM) is explicitly defined in the legend
assertions.note: Error bar definition present per checklist requirement.

assertions.text: Legend does not fabricate statistical results not provided by the user
assertions.note: Hard rules enforced; no invented p-values or group differences.

inputs.label: 3-panel figure: (A) western blot, (B) bar graph quantification, (C) microscopy — loading control not specified
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Per-panel structure applied; loading control placeholder correctly inserted.

assertions.text: Output addresses each panel separately using (A), (B), (C) structure
assertions.note: Per-panel format applied as specified in Step 2 multi-panel guidance.

assertions.text: Western blot panel legend includes loading control placeholder
assertions.note: [AUTHOR: specify loading control] placeholder inserted rather than inventing a loading control.

assertions.text: Microscopy panel legend includes scale bar placeholder
assertions.note: [AUTHOR: specify scale bar and magnification] placeholder correctly inserted.

assertions.text: Step 4 output notes all placeholders that need to be filled before submission
Legend does not fabricate the loading control protein or scale bar value
assertions.note: Hard rules enforced throughout multi-panel output.

inputs.label: Minimal input: 'Write a legend for my figure' — no figure type or details
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Figure type and critical details requested before proceeding; no legend produced.

assertions.text: Skill does not produce a legend without knowing the figure type
assertions.note: Input validation triggered; figure type requested first.

assertions.text: Output asks for figure type, sample size, statistical test, and error bar type
assertions.note: All critical missing inputs requested in Step 1 format.

assertions.text: Output does not invent a figure description or placeholder legend
assertions.note: Hard rules enforced; no fabricated figure content.

assertions.text: Output lists the universal elements that will be needed regardless of figure type
assertions.note: User informed of required inputs up front.

assertions.text: Output communicates that type-specific elements will be applied once figure type is known
assertions.note: Progressive disclosure: user informed of what differentiates legend by figure type.

key_strengths: 11-figure-type coverage with type-specific additional elements is the most comprehensive figure legend framework in this skill family
Standardized placeholder convention ([n = X per group], [AUTHOR: specify error bar type]) prevents fabrication while keeping the output usable
Journal-specific requirements (Nature, Cell Press, Science) in academic_style_guide.md enable direct journal-targeted output
8 legend templates in legend_templates.md provide copy-ready starting points for the most common figure types

recommendations.title: Journal-specific word limits and requirements in academic_style_guide.md will go stale without version tracking
recommendations.problem: The Nature Series 350-word limit, Cell Press formatting requirements, and Science style preferences change periodically. The reference file has no 'last verified' date, making it impossible to know if the guidelines are current.
recommendations.root_cause: No version tracking or update reminder in the reference file.
recommendations.fix: Add a 'last-verified' date field to the top of academic_style_guide.md and a note: 'Verify current journal requirements at the official author guidelines page before submission. Requirements change with journal guide updates.'

recommendations.title: No guidance for multi-panel figures where panels share statistical context
recommendations.problem: When panels (A) and (B) show the same experiment at different representations (e.g., representative blot and quantification bar), the statistical notation and sample size should appear once in a shared note, not separately for each panel. The skill currently produces redundant statistical declarations.
recommendations.root_cause: Multi-panel format guidance in Step 2 addresses each panel separately without a shared-context mechanism.
recommendations.fix: Add to Step 2: 'For multi-panel figures where panels share the same experiment or sample (e.g., representative image and quantification), state sample size and statistics once in a shared note after the last panel description: All panels: n=[N] per group; statistical test; significance thresholds.'

recommendations.title: No distinction between main figure legends and supplementary figure legends
recommendations.problem: Some journals have different length or formatting requirements for supplementary figure legends vs. main figure legends. The skill does not ask whether the legend is for a main or supplementary figure.
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation does not include figure location (main vs. supplementary).
recommendations.fix: Add to Step 1: 'Is this legend for a main figure or a supplementary figure? Some journals have shorter length limits or different formatting rules for supplementary legends.'
